title: RoutingDecisionEvent
description: F036 routing.decision.v1 — render-contract for routing decisions emitted by a registered routing.Decider implementation. The OSS distribution stores and renders these events verbatim. The OSS no-op decider (packages/extensions/go/routing) emits a single trivial reason step. Registered deciders may emit rich reason_chain and alternatives content; OSS treats those fields as opaque JSON. This schema deliberately specifies SHAPE only — it does NOT define scoring, weighting, ranking, or selection semantics. Those are intentionally not defined by this schema.

id.description: Globally unique decision identifier (UUIDv7 recommended). Idempotency key for ledger ingest.

tenant_id.description: Tenant UUID. Every decision is owned by exactly one tenant.

team.description: Team label as carried on the originating request. Free-form; never enriched server-side.

app.description: App label as carried on the originating request.

project.description: Project label as carried on the originating request.

request.description: Minimal request snapshot. NEVER contains prompt text, completion text, request headers other than the request-id, or any caller payload.

provider_requested.description: Provider the caller asked for (e.g. openai, anthropic, gemini, azure_openai, bedrock).

model_requested.description: Model the caller asked for, as written by the caller.

route.description: Logical route the caller used (e.g. /v1/chat/completions). Free-form.

request_id_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the inbound X-Request-ID header, or empty if the caller did not supply one. Never the raw header.

decision.description: The decider's output: which provider/model was selected. The selection logic itself is owned by the registered routing.Decider implementation and is not described here.

route_chosen.description: Resolved upstream route, if different from request.route. Free-form.

reason_chain.description: Ordered list of factors the decider considered. The OSS layer stores this array verbatim and renders it as a tree. Field semantics are owned by the decider — OSS does NOT interpret factor names, values, thresholds, or weight_hint numbers. The OSS no-op decider emits a single step like {step:1, factor:"noop", value:"identity"}.

factor.description: Decider-defined factor name. Treated as an opaque label by OSS.

value.description: Decider-defined factor value. May be a string, number, boolean, array, or object. Treated as an opaque blob by OSS.

threshold.description: Decider-defined threshold value. Treated as an opaque blob by OSS.

weight_hint.description: Decider-defined hint number. OSS stores and displays this number verbatim and does NOT interpret it as a weight, score, or coefficient.

alternatives.description: Provider/model candidates the decider considered but did not select. The OSS layer stores this array verbatim. rejected_because is a free-form tag, not an algorithm description.

score_hint.description: Decider-defined hint number. OSS stores and displays this number verbatim and does NOT interpret it as a score or ranking position.

rejected_because.description: Decider-defined tag explaining why this candidate was not chosen. Treated as an opaque label by OSS.

decided_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp the decider produced this decision.

decider_version.description: Version identifier of the registered decider that produced this decision. The OSS no-op decider reports "oss-noop"; registered deciders report their own version string. OSS does not parse or compare these strings.

trace_id.description: W3C trace context trace-id, if available.
